event: Early human ancestors inhabit regions of present-day Germany, as evidenced by the Heidelberg Man jaw found near Mauer.
Anatomically modern humans living in the Swabian Jura carve the Lion-man and other early figurative art and instruments.
Neolithic farmers of the Linear Pottery culture introduce agriculture and permanent villages across the fertile loess lands.
The Nebra Sky Disc, one of the oldest known depictions of the cosmos, is crafted during the Bronze Age in Saxony-Anhalt.
Migrating Cimbri and Teutones clash with Rome, beginning recorded contact between Germanic peoples and the Mediterranean world.
Germanic tribes led by Arminius annihilate three Roman legions at the Battle of the Teutoburg Forest, halting Rome's expansion east of the Rhine.
Germanic warlord Odoacer deposes the last Western Roman emperor, accelerating the rise of Germanic kingdoms.
Charlemagne is crowned emperor in Rome, uniting much of the German-speaking lands under Carolingian rule.
The Treaty of Verdun divides the Carolingian Empire, creating East Francia as the nucleus of the future German state.
Henry the Fowler becomes king of East Francia, founding the Ottonian dynasty that consolidated the German realm.
Otto I is crowned emperor in Rome, establishing the Holy Roman Empire centered on the German lands.
Emperor Henry IV submits to Pope Gregory VII at Canossa during the Investiture Controversy over church and imperial authority.
The Golden Bull issued by Charles IV formalizes the system of seven prince-electors choosing the Holy Roman Emperor.
Johannes Gutenberg perfects movable-type printing in Mainz, launching the print revolution in Europe.
Martin Luther posts his Ninety-five Theses in Wittenberg, igniting the Protestant Reformation.
The German Peasants' War erupts as commoners rise against feudal lords before being brutally suppressed.
The Peace of Augsburg lets each German prince choose Lutheranism or Catholicism for his territory.
The Defenestration of Prague triggers the Thirty Years' War, which devastates the German lands.
The Peace of Westphalia ends the Thirty Years' War, entrenching the fragmentation of the Holy Roman Empire.
The Duchy of Prussia is elevated to a kingdom, beginning Prussia's rise as a major German power.
Frederick the Great ascends the Prussian throne and seizes Silesia, expanding Prussian power in Central Europe.
Napoleon dissolves the Holy Roman Empire after defeating Austria and Prussia, reorganizing the German states.
The Congress of Vienna creates the German Confederation, a loose league of 39 states under Austrian leadership.
The Zollverein customs union removes trade barriers among German states, advancing economic unification.
Liberal revolutions sweep the German states and the Frankfurt Parliament attempts to unify the nation.
Otto von Bismarck unifies Germany under Prussian leadership, proclaiming the German Empire at Versailles after victory over France.
Germany enters World War I, joining the Central Powers against the Allied nations.
Germany's defeat in World War I ends the monarchy as the Kaiser abdicates and a republic is proclaimed.
The Treaty of Versailles imposes harsh terms on Germany, and the democratic Weimar Republic is founded.
Hyperinflation devastates the German economy as the mark collapses to near-worthlessness.
Adolf Hitler becomes chancellor and the Nazi Party establishes a totalitarian dictatorship.
Germany invades Poland, igniting World War II in Europe.
Germany launches the invasion of the Soviet Union and escalates the systematic genocide of the Holocaust.
Nazi Germany surrenders unconditionally, ending World War II in Europe and leaving the country occupied and in ruins.
Germany is split into the democratic Federal Republic (West) and the communist German Democratic Republic (East).
West Germany joins NATO while East Germany joins the Warsaw Pact, formalizing Cold War division.
West Germany becomes a founding member of the European Economic Community via the Treaty of Rome.
East Germany builds the Berlin Wall, sealing off West Berlin and symbolizing the divided nation.
Both West and East Germany are admitted to the United Nations.
Peaceful protests and the fall of the Berlin Wall bring down the East German regime.
Germany is officially reunified, dissolving the GDR and merging East into the Federal Republic.
Germany adopts the euro for accounting as a founding member of the eurozone.
Euro banknotes and coins replace the Deutsche Mark in everyday circulation.
Angela Merkel becomes Germany's first female chancellor, beginning sixteen years in office.
Germany receives over a million refugees during the European migrant crisis, reshaping its politics and society.
Olaf Scholz succeeds Angela Merkel as chancellor, leading a new three-party coalition government.
Germany overhauls its defense and energy policies in response to Russia's invasion of Ukraine.
